headline: Midstate Wrestlers Inducted into PA Hall of Fame; Balum Battles Virus in Tournament
summary: The article reports on the Pennsylvania Wrestling Coaches Association Hall of Fame induction ceremony, while also detailing the ongoing struggles and competitive efforts of wrestler Balum during a local tournament.
cleaned_text:
A Trio of Midstate Wrestlers to Join Pennsylvania Hall of Fame

HALL OF FAME INDUCTIONS: A trio of former wrestlers with midstate ties will be among the people inducted into the Pennsylvania Wrestling Coaches Association 2006 Hall of Fame class. The ceremony, to be held tonight at Giant Center, includes inductees Dave Lum, Steve Sefter, and Dave Hart.

McCollum, the head coach of Bermudian Springs, wrestled at Susquehanna Twp. in the early 1970s and later at Bloomsburg. He has produced 51 state qualifiers and one champion, Trevor Byers.

Sefter, a former Cedar Cliff High School standout and 1980 graduate, was a four-time district champion.

The action was also highlighted by the struggles of Balum, a Virginia recruit who won at 130 last year. Balum admitted that an unrelenting virus had been his toughest obstacle in the tournament. He described his recovery, stating, “After regionals, I got sick, I think it was last Monday... I went to the doctor Monday, and he said that it might be a virus, and it needed to work itself out.”

Despite the illness, Balum continued competing, wobbling through his final two minutes with Struening, allowing an uncharacteristic takedown with 1:25 left before holding on for a two-point victory.

The championship round was scheduled to begin at 12:30, preceded by the third, fifth, and seventh-place bouts. In his final match of the day, Balum faced Hamburg’s unbeaten senior, Nick Stabile (45-0).

title: The Patriot-News
headline: Midstate Wrestling Legends Inducted into Hall of Fame
summary: The article reports on the induction of wrestling coaches and athletes into the Pennsylvania Coaches Association Hall of Fame while also providing an update on the ongoing state wrestling tournament.
cleaned_text:
The Patriot-News
Sat, Mar 11, 2006

**HALL OF FAME INDUCTIONS**
A trio of former midstate wrestlers will be among the inductees into the Pennsylvania Coaches Association 2006 Hall of Fame. The ceremony, held that evening at Giant Center, honored several notable figures.

Among the inductees is McCollum, the head coach from Bermudian Springs. McCollum wrestled at Susquehanna Twp. in the early 1970s and later at Bloomsburg. He has a distinguished record, having produced 51 state qualifiers and one state champion, Trevor Byers.

Another inductee is Steve Sefter, a former Cedar Cliff High School standout and 1980 graduate. Sefter was a four-time District 3 champion and a two-time state runner-up.

**WRESTLING TOURNAMENT UPDATE**
The championship round of the wrestling tournament began at 2:30 p.m., preceded by the third, fifth, and seventh-place bouts, with action starting at 12:30 p.m.

The tournament featured several key matchups. In one bout, the unbeaten Eagles’ senior wobbled through his final two minutes with Struening, allowing an uncharacteristic takedown with 1:25 left before holding on for a two-point victory.

Meanwhile, Balum, a Virginia recruit who won at 130 last year, spoke about his participation. He explained that he had been battling an unrelenting virus, which was his toughest obstacle to date. "After regionals, I got sick, I think it was last Monday," Balum said. "I went to the doctor Monday, and he said that it might be a virus, and it needed to work itself out. I actually felt better this morning, then I took a little nap today. It had been getting better every day, but two matches in one day, it didn’t go too good."

Balum was scheduled to compete in a final match against Hamburg’s unbeaten senior Nick Stabile (45-0), who had defeated Ridgway Iby Pisani in the 135 semifinal.

title: weird
headline: Bermudian Springs Wrestler Schmelyun Faces Boos at PIAA Championships
summary: Bermudian Springs wrestler Rickey Schmelyun advanced to the PIAA championship bout at 140 pounds, facing boos from the crowd despite his victory, while other wrestlers like Dahlheimer also competed in the tournament.
cleaned_text:
Dahlheimer (33-2) kicked his effort up a notch. Trailing 42, Dahlheimer rode the Southwest regional champion for the entire two minutes to stay in the bout. After getting his escape early in the third to make it 43, the District 3 runner-up kept the lead on a takedown with less than 20 seconds left. The problem was the YAIAA wrestler had a stall call, so he couldn't just hang on. But that’s exactly what he found himself doing for the last five seconds of the match, holding on to Byerly’s lower leg in a position often called stalling—but not this time.

“That's a situation you never want to be in. I had to kind of make it look like I was trying to turn him,” said Dahlheimer. “I could hear the assistant ref counting down, ‘five, four, three,’ so I dropped down to the leg.”

With a medal secured, Dahlheimer can now face the challenge of dethroning a champion without any pressure. His semi-final foe is Nazareth's Tim Darling (43-2), who is up three weight classes from 2005, when he claimed the 130-pound title.

headline: Local Wrestlers Claim State Titles
summary: Local wrestlers achieved state championship titles, with Rickey Schmelyun winning the 140-pound division and Polashuk also claiming a state title.
cleaned_text:
The text reports on several state championship wrestling victories, highlighting the performances of local athletes.

In one notable victory, Rickey Schmelyun secured the 140-pound state championship title after defeating Aaron Nestor of Reynolds. Following the match, Schmelyun received congratulations from his coach.

Another championship victory was achieved by Polashuk, who won state titles representing Bermudian Springs. Polashuk described his strategy in the match, noting that he focused on attempting takedowns to maintain control, especially when facing stalling calls.

Coach McCollum provided commentary on the competitive nature of the sport, stating that wrestling requires a smart wrestler and the ability to capitalize on opportunities.

The article also mentions other athletes and results, including Josh Wh, Trewn Cowman, and results from the Eagle Area and Ambridge.

headline: Penalty Point Decides Thrilling Wrestling Match
summary: A wrestling match was decided by a penalty point awarded in the second period after the wrestlers became tangled from neutral.
cleaned_text:
The wrestling match was decided by a dramatic penalty point awarded in the second period. The referee ruled that the difference in the score was due to a penalty point, rather than the action itself. During the period, the wrestlers became tangled from neutral. With the opponent searching for a takedown, the referee called a penalty for an illegal leg action, which resulted in a point. This penalty point ultimately tied the score and proved to be the deciding factor in the bout.

title: <7 “Dave h
headline: State Champions Crowned at PIAA Wrestling Championships
summary: The PIAA Championships concluded with multiple wrestlers achieving state titles and top-five finishes, highlighting the season's success for several Pennsylvania high school teams.
cleaned_text:
The PIAA Championships Showcase State Wrestling Success

Sophomore Haynes, weighing 189 pounds, spoke about the success of his team, noting that after making it to the state championships, he realized he could compete at the state level. Haynes was joined on the podium by Red Tornadoes sophomore Josh Malick, who won a seventh-place consolation match at 103 pounds by major decision. Haynes credited his success to working out with former Red Tornadoes placewinner Eric Lapotsky.

The PIAA Championships concluded with several teams placing in the top five in their respective weight classes, including state championships for Schmelyun and Warrior Run.

Warrior Run’s Jason Cuffey secured his second Class AA state title at 112 pounds. He had previously won the 103-pound title as a junior the year before.

In another notable match, Schmelyun was taken to its back in the second period by Reynolds’ Aaron Nestor, who had initially built a 10-0 lead in the first period with three sets of back points. Nestor fought off a late rally to finish the season 44-0.

Other state champions included Holidaysburg’s Brian Shaw, Matt Dunn (145 pounds), and Andy Rendos (152 pounds). Dunn, a senior, won the 103-pound title with a technical fall, marking a significant victory as he had not won the title since his freshman year.

headline: Local Athletes Celebrate Wins and Scholarships

headline: Dallastown Wildcats Claim Division Crown; Bermudian Springs Recognized
summary: The Dallastown Wildcats won their first division crown since 2000, while Bermudian Springs was recognized for its coaching, and several other local wrestlers achieved sectional and state success.
cleaned_text:
Dallastown Wildcats Claim First Division Crown Since 2000

Dallastown Wildcats went 8-0 in the YAIAA tournament to claim their first out-of-division crown since 2000. The team then reached the sectional semifinals and the state consolation finals.

In other local wrestling news, the Bermudian Springs Eagles were highlighted by coach McCollum, who received an honor for the district year. The Eagles breezed through Division II with a 10-0 record (13-2 overall) and advanced to the district team semifinals. The team's success was bolstered by Rickey Schmelyun's state championship victory at 140 pounds.

Several other wrestlers had standout seasons. At Dallastown, the Wildcats' team continued its strong run, with one wrestler finishing the season with a 33-10 mark.

Central York's Zarfos had a strong showing, reaching the Class AA district final at 103 pounds, where he lost to the top-ranked wrestler.

In the Class AAA division, the Wildcats' team reached the sectional tournament final, going 21-10 with a silver medal in the 152-pound sectional tournament.

Hanover's Thomas Slaugh, a senior, went 40-7 with a Class AA 152-pound sectional crown, leading the Night-hawks to the Class AA district team finals.

Another standout from Hanover was Wolford, who, despite narrowly missing out on states, won the Class AA 119-pound district title during a 37-12 season.

Eastern York's Ryan Smith finished an outstanding four-year career with a sixth-place state medal in Class AA at 119 pounds, defeating fellow all-star Brown. He had previously won a sectional crown before dropping three straight matches to his rival, ending with a 40-6 record.

Meanwhile, Bermudian Springs' McCollum received recognition for his coaching efforts.

title: “Dave
headline: West York Wrestling Program Focuses on Rebuilding Foundation
summary: West York's new coach, Millard, emphasized that the wrestling program's focus is on building a strong foundation and developing effort, while the article also detailed team achievements and state weight certification rules.
cleaned_text:
Millard, the new head coach at West York, expressed excitement about the wrestling program's future. "I'm even more glad to be able to help; I know the program needed help. I'm happy to get the program up and running," Millard said, whose coaching career began at the Brandywine Heights Bullet Invitational this weekend. While he acknowledges that the rebuilding project will take time, he stated, "The clock is ticking, but I'm not in a rush. The foundation is the most important part. Separately, the text addressed the technical aspects of state competition, detailing the process by which wrestlers become certified for weight classes. Wrestlers must adhere to strict weight guidelines, ensuring they do not fall below their minimum body weight. Competition requires wrestlers to lose no more than 1.5 percent of their body weight. Proper certification and preparation are crucial for success in state competition.

title: The Evening Sun
headline: Spring Grove Claims Girls' Wrestling Championship Amid Rule Changes
summary: The article reports on updated wrestling rules, Spring Grove's championship success, and a local story about a hunter's first deer kill.
cleaned_text:
**The Evening Sun**
**Sun, Dec 03, 2006**

**Wrestling Rules and Local Sports Updates**

**Weight Testing and Athletic Standards**
Hanover coach Terry Conover discussed the importance of athletic testing, noting that while the skin fold test is standard, the hydration test provides an additional opportunity for athletes to measure their body composition multiple times during the season.

**Wrestling Rule Changes**
In addition to new weight rules, which require athletes to eat and lift weights to ensure they are not too small for their weight class, there are other adjustments for wrestlers. Overtime rules are being updated to match the college format. After the first one-minute overtime period, there will now be two 30-second rideouts, or tiebreakers, instead of one. The scores from these two rideouts will be combined. If the match is still tied, there will be a final 30-second rideout.

McCollum, who is set to begin his 30th season as head coach, stated that while the changes might seem complex, "everybody gets used to this stuff, it will be a piece of cake."

**Spring Grove Wrestling Success**
At a recent invitational meet, Spring Grove claimed the girls’ championship. The Lady Rockets, led by Kelsie Jone and Carolyn Rohrbaugh, secured second place in both the 200m and 400 freestyle relays, and also took second in the backstroke. In the boys’ competition, Spring Grove placed fourth, while Dover won the meet. Amie Mow was noted for her victory in the 200-yard individual medley.

**Local Hunting News**
In unrelated news, Cody Wood of Mount Holly Springs recently shot his first deer, a 4-point buck, while hunting on his uncle’s Adams County farm. The deer weighed about 120 pounds.

title: 1 of 1
headline: Bermudian Springs Falls to Boiling Springs in Third Straight Loss
summary: Bermudian Springs suffered its third consecutive wrestling loss to Boiling Springs, highlighted by a final bout loss for Nate Rill to Anthony Wieland.
cleaned_text:
Bermudian Springs Lost Third Straight Wrestling Match to Boiling Springs, 28-26

By GREG SWATEK
For the Daily Record/Sunday News

BOILING SPRINGS — Bermudian Springs is still searching for a way to beat Boiling Springs on a mat.

The Eagles lost their third match in two seasons to the Bubblers on Monday night when Anthony Wieland pinned Bermudian Springs’ Nate Rill in the final bout at 160 pounds, giving the defending District 3 Class AA champions a dramatic 28-26 victory before a boisterous home crowd.

It marks the second straight time that Bermudian Springs has lost its season opener to Boiling Springs. The team had previously dropped at home to the Bubblers in last year's season opener, and was again beaten by Boiling Springs in the District 3 Class AA semifinals.

This most recent loss did not sting as much for Bermudian Springs head coach McCollum. "I thought we wrestled an even match for it being the first match of the season," McCollum said, coming off the mat a little wounded.

During the match, Rill had started strong, grabbing a 6-3 lead on Wieland with a pair of takedowns and a reversal. However, Wieland started reversing the momentum in the final seconds of the first period with an escape. He moved ahead of Rill in the second period with four back points and then turned him on his back with a powerful half-nelson for the pin.

Earlier in the night, Dustin Eyler gave the first lead of the night when he pinned Kyle Ross in 25 seconds. The Eagles trailed 16-4 after five bouts. Nick Blasone started a comeback effort with a win at 112, and Bryce Marshall followed with a 15-1 rout at 119. The senior’s major decision closed the gap to 16-11, and Peters pulled Bermudian Springs within points with a 6-3 decision at 125. The Bubblers held off the visit’s first challenge with consecutive decisions at 130 and 135 to build their lead eight points with four bouts remaining. Brian Ford started the Eagles’ comeback with a 6-3 decision, and McCreary won an 8-5 decision, bringing the score to 22-20. Eyler’s dominant 160-pound performance gave Bermudian Springs its only lead of the night.

headline: Central and Bermudian Springs Athletes Shine in Local Sports Victories
summary: The article reports on a victory for the Panthers and highlights the Bermudian Springs wrestling team's continued success, noting their seventh consecutive win.
cleaned_text:
**Central and Panthers Win; Bermudian Springs Continues Wrestling Streak**

The York Daily Record reported on local sports action, noting a victory for Central. In a game played Tuesday, the Panthers were highlighted by Gillespie, who led the team with 12 points. Coach Wisner praised Gillespie's performance, stating, “When she gets going, she’s tough to stop. She's incredible; she has the mentality that every time she shoots the ball, it is going in the basket. She really stepped up and gave us a lift offensively and gave us valuable minutes.” Central led 21 to start the third quarter, and Kim Podolsky’s 3-pointer from the right wing with 4:12 remaining in the quarter pushed the Panthers' lead to 27-22.

In other sports news, the Bermudian Springs Eagles continued their successful run. The team secured a 45-24 victory against visitors in York Springs, racking up bonus points in seven of their nine match wins. This win kept the Eagles on track for next week's divisional showdown with Hanover.

At the wrestling mat, the Eagles maintained their impressive streak. Devin O'Brien (135 pounds), Derek Schmelyun (140 pounds), and William Marshall (108 pounds) each scored pins in less than a minute to go, along with two other pins from Nick Grim (152 pounds) and Stanton (189 pounds). This marked Bermudian Springs' seventh consecutive win and their tenth in 12 matches.

Eagles coach McCollum praised the team's effort, noting that the sophomore duo of O'Brien and Schmelyun, coupled with Ron McCreary's decision at 145 pounds, were important since they came in the first three bouts of the evening. “Devin and Derek did an excellent job, and Ron wrestled a solid match. We came out of the blocks strong, which we had to do,” McCollum said. “We knew they would be trouble up top.”

**Bermudian Springs Eagles Can Take Outright YAIAA II Crown Saturday**

By Greg Swatke
Evergreen Sun Sports Writer

Bermudian Springs High School is defending its own crown in the tournament, staking its sights on salvaging its eventual championship. While the team is still a match or two away from perfecting its dual-match lineup, the Eagles are poised to compete for the YAIAA II division title.

Bermudian Springs (12-2, 9-9) has a chance to wrap up the division championship this spring and was honored in a brief ceremony prior to the final.

Meanwhile, neighboring McLain High School secured at least a share of their second straight YAIAA II division II title Thursday night.

In other sports news, Hanover High School celebrated four wins by its 300th high school team. Coach Ralph Ruth, who has coached the team, decided to skip away after coaching his final dual match night a third time this spring, and was honored in a brief ceremony prior to the final.

cleaned_text:
**YORK DAILY RECORD**
**FRIDAY, JANUARY 26, 2007**

**SPORTS**

**HIGH SCHOOL WRESTLING**

**Tri-Champions Crowned in YAIAA Division I**

The wrestling season concluded with an increasingly familiar conclusion to the YAIAA Division I campaign: three-way champions. Dallastown, Red Lion, and South Wester all won their matches to finish tied atop the division with 7-1 records. This marks the third time in four seasons that Division I has had tri-champions.

The Wildcats of Dallastown, who were undefeated champions last year, were unable to repeat the feat. The streak was broken only by Dallastown’s undefeated championship season last year, and the Wildcats have been unable to repeat the feat.

In other news, the Eagles of Hanover secured a victory that sets up a final league showdown at Biglerville on Saturday. The Canners could share a Division I title with a win against Bermudian Springs.

Earlier in the week, Hanover’s Justin Boyers battled Bermudian Springs’ Bryant Peters in their 125-pound bout on Thursday night at Hanover. Boyers scored a 10-7 decision, but the Eagles prevailed, 38-16, clinching at least a tie for the YAIAA Division II crown.

With crucial league matches against Hanover and Biglerville this week, one would think Bermudian Springs wrestling coach Dave McCollum would have his Eagles in the practice room as much as possible. One would be wrong.

For Red Lion, it is the first championship since the Lions shared the title with Spring Grove and Jallastown in 2004-05. For South Wester, it is the championship since the Mustangs won outright in 1996-97.

title: Matt
headline: Local Teams Compete in High-Stakes Wrestling Tournaments
summary: Multiple high school wrestling tournaments were held on February 2, 2007, featuring decisive victories and technical falls across various weight classes.
cleaned_text:
The wrestling circuit saw several high-stakes matches on February 2, 2007, featuring multiple regional tournaments.

In one major bout, Chris Albright of the Lions boosted the seventh-seeded team early with a technical fall over Gavin Reilly in the 112-pound bout. However, the lead was established by Luke Walker, who gave Cumberland Valley the lead for good with a 94-point decision over Jerrid Hartman at 112 pounds. The Eagles continued their success, with Tom Wiechelt pinning Ryan Luu in the third period, and Joey Napoli earning a forfeit, giving the hosts a 23-5 advantage.

Red Lion’s Bobby Doyle earned a +1 decision over a competitor, while Stan Warner pinned Collin Snyder in the second period, and Mike Evans scored a technical fall over Dylan Miller in the 152-pound match. The Eagles put the final touches on their win as E. Red, Jake Martin, and De Hockensmith earned consecutive pins at 171, 189, and 215 pounds. Separately, the Lions’ Kevin Kriner provided a highlight by pinning Travis Friend in the 103-pound bout.

In another tournament, Nick Blasone helped Bermudian Springs secure a victory with his own pin with 41 seconds left in the match. Hamburg retook the lead when Billy King earned a 10-1 major decision over Bryant Peters at 125 pounds. Marshall gave the team an 119-pound lead, and Brad Kummerer secured a technical fall in the next match. Coach McCollum advised his team, stating, “It’s panic. They just kept plugging away.”

Meanwhile, Central Dauphin held down the 103- through 145-pound bouts, while the Wildcats’ Casey Bogert scored a takedown. Mike Myel later secured a pin at 215 pounds.

In separate action, the Rams defeated the Wildcats in a Class AAA team quarterfinal with a score of 38-25.

cleaned_text:
By GREG SWATEK
Evening Sun Sports Writer

Bermudian Springs Eagles Fall to Bubblers in District 3 Class AA Tournament Final

An extraordinary day of highs and lows ended on a pretty flat note for Bermudian Springs. The Eagles fell to the defending champion Bubblers with a 32-31 loss in the final of the District 3 Class AA Team Tournament on Saturday.

Bermudian Springs, coached by McCollum, was on the verge of achieving a major victory. The team fought hard, but the Bubblers secured their second straight district title.

The final eight bouts were highly competitive. The Bubblers claimed victory through several key moments, including a momentum-turning fall by Travis Simpson over Matt Spahr at the 215 weight class.

Despite the loss, the Eagles showed a mighty effort. Senior standout Bryce Marshall was a key contributor, scoring an 8-0 major decision over Mark Hammond at the 119 weight class.

title: a Gettysburg High alumna,
headline: Local Athletes Compete in Swimmer Records and PIAA Wrestling Tournament
summary: The article reports on a local swimmer's record-breaking performance and provides updates on the intense competition faced by Hanover area wrestling teams in the PIAA Team Tournament.
cleaned_text:
A Gettysburg High alumna was named the 2007 Atlantic Women’s Colleges Conference Most Outstanding Swimmer after helping the Gators to a second-place finish at the AWCC Championships.

**COLLEGE CORNER**

Ackerman won three individual events at the meet, two on the second day. She also set two AWCC Championships meet records: a school record and a pool record at the competition at Chatham College.

On Sunday, Ackerman won the 200-yard backstroke by more than three seconds, clocking a time of 2:24.38 to break the pool record set by Mary Baldwin's Shona Fenner in 2006. Ackerman came back three events later to win the 100-yard backstroke with a time of 1:05.66, breaking the record of 1:05.80 set by Notre Dame's Becca Parrow in 2004.

**THE EVENING SUN**

The long road to Somerset County and the small towns south of Johnstown has been well traveled by Hanover area wrestling teams over the past seasons. For the District 3 Class AA runner-up title, worn by Delone Catholic two years ago and Bermudian Springs this year, it is the only route back to Hershey in the PIAA Team Tournament.

Except for Delone and Hanover, no other team has made it that far. Their trips were cut short when they met the Cougars (18-4) in a PIAA first-round match at 7 p.m. Tuesday at Shade High School in Cairnbrook, located about 20 miles southeast of Johnstown.

The 135-mile trip is not entirely unfamiliar to the Eagles, who wrestle in the nearby Penn Cambria Tournament every year.

“The tip is not any different than we normally do. We check into a hotel and relax for a few hours before going to wrestle. We get up there about three or four hours early and then go hit a mall to unwind a little bit before,” said a source.

McCollum expects North Star to be a pretty formidable opponent. The Cougars have won the last four District 5 titles, including upset victories over the top seed in the last two district finals.

The last name Lascari has been synonymous with North Star's success in recent years, and this season is no exception. Freshman Joe Lascari is the top-ranked 103-pounder and ranked No. 6 at 125 by the same site.

“I know they have to program, being champs for four years. McCollum knows Delone and Hanover have gone up there and had a tough time. We're not pinning on the same thing,” said a source.

headline: Bermuda Springs Dominates PIAA Class AA Team Wrestling Championship

cleaned_text:
Seth Snyder set the tone for Bermuda at the first round of the PIAA Class AA Team Wrestling Championship on Tuesday at Shade Central City High School. In the opening match at 120 pounds, Snyder secured a fall over a wrestler from Zimmerman, and the Bermuda Eagles followed that lead with three more bouts in fall, leading to a dominating 44-18 victory over the District 5 champion Cougier.

"We came out strong in Bermuda. We won both McCullum and it was a huge win there by Seth Snyder at 130. We fought tough against Zimmerman," one source noted. "That set the tone for our team the rest of the night. I think the law at that point that we had the upper hand again with that pin."

The loss denied North Star a fourth straight trip to Henryville to continue on a tour.

In other matches, Nippon Eagles led North Star to win two weight classes in Lauren Paul Leewen (7-2), while Aaron Delano defeated Bermuda (5-2) at 189 pounds. Bermuda Springs subsequently won the next six bouts, including three decisions, to seal their overall victory.

"We felt with North Star the way they win it up here; we knew back then to finish it," another source stated.

cleaned_text:
**Varsity Wrestling and Area Sports Roundup**

**Varsity Wrestling Results**
In the varsity wrestling match, Biglerville defeated Gettysburg. The match featured several key victories across multiple weight classes:

*   **125 lbs:** Bryan Orendort (G) won by forfeit.
*   **130 lbs:** Nic Lieberum (B) pinned Sean Shelleman at 3:19.
*   **135 lbs:** Nick Asper (B) pinned Brandon Vogelsong at 1:10.
*   **140 lbs:** Shay Shive (B) pinned Kenneth Hartlaub at :11.
*   **145 lbs:** Logan Bobo (B) won by forfeit.
*   **152 lbs:** Brandon Brookens (G) secured a major decision over Seth Shive (10-1).
*   **160 lbs:** Kyle Grubbs (B) won by decision over Cory McCleal (5-4).
*   **171 lbs:** Bryce Shatfer (G) won by decision over Danny Thomas (2-0).
*   **189 lbs:** Jordan King (B) achieved a technical fall against Jordan Martin (4:41, 16-0).
*   **215 lbs:** Jett Foster (B) pinned Zack Mencl at 1:10.
*   **103 lbs:** Justin Amato (G) won by major decision over Dustin Bower (19-10).
*   **112 lbs:** Eric Pelo (B) won by forfeit.
*   **119 lbs:** Ryan Warner (G) won by major decision.

**Other Sports Highlights**
In girls' basketball, Hilary Rice scored 14 points, including two three-pointers, leading the Lady Canners (5-18) in a non-league game. Chelsea Cool added 10 points. Keyonna Montgomery led Scotland with 14 points.

At New Oxford, Autumn Lau tallied 33 points, pacing the Lady Bobcats, while Erin O'Brien scored 13 points for the Lady Colonials.

In boys' basketball, the Rockets (8-16) fell at Carlisle, despite 15 points from Zach Markle.

In soccer, the Italian Cabinet approved measures that could force top league teams to play in empty stadiums, banning clubs from selling blocks of tickets to visiting fans and allowing authorities to bar suspected hooligans.

The Eagles at Cairnbrook were featured as Berwick made its first appearance in the PIAA Team Tournament, defeating Lake Lehman 46-17 in the District 2 championship match. Their lineup is anchored by Aaron Karns, who is ranked No. 4 in the state.

The YAIAA playoffs continued with Delone Catholic girls facing Red Lion, and the boys' teams also meeting. Spring Grove faced Northeastern at Dover, and Biglerville faced West York at Dover.

In swimming, Tricia Weaner competed in the Mid-Penn Conference Class Championship.

title: teams stood as
headline: District 2 Title Winners Head to PIAA Wrestling Championships
summary: Several high school wrestling teams, including Bermudian Springs and Hazleton, are preparing for highly anticipated matchups at the PIAA team wrestling championships in Hershey.
cleaned_text:
The District 2 title winners will take the mat at the GIANT Center in Hershey for the PIAA team wrestling championships. While every team has a different path through the bracket in the second round of competition, the matchups are highly anticipated.

In the AAA race, Hazleton faces perhaps the toughest of the two challenges, taking on District 3 runner-up Cumberland Valley. The Eagles defeated District 4 champion Shikel-Kennedy.

In a separate matchup, the Flyers are on to the Maple Leafs.

Should the Cougars defeat Cumberland Valley, it will be the first time they face a more daunting challenge in today’s quarter.

Bermudian Springs, with a District 11 title and history on their side, has won four of the seven AAA team titles.

In a strange parallel on the AA side, Berwick also faces the District 3 runner-up, who also happens to be the Eagles.

Bermudian Springs went 8-0 in the York Adams Interscholastic Athletic Association season, and are 17-3 overall.

Berwick (18-6) coach Ernie Yates said he doesn’t know much about the Eagles, though he knows their coach. They wrestle Bloomsburg, and though he said they’ve seen each other occasionally at meets, this will be the first time their teams will face off.

“That'll be kind of interesting,” Yates said.

If he gets past his former teammate, his Bulldogs will go on to face District 10 and defending AA champion McCollum.

headline: Flickinger's Dedication Fuels NCAA Wrestling Success
summary: Kyle Flickinger's success at the NCAA Division II Wrestling Championships is attributed to his intense work ethic, dedication, and ability to absorb coaching advice.
cleaned_text:
Sophomore Kyle Flickinger was often found engaging in playful mayhem, though his dedication to wrestling was undeniable. While senior Tim Bohlman once recalled Flickinger's initial reluctance to participate, Bohlman later noted that it took a lot to get him to open up and join the fun.

Two seasons later, Flickinger was a dedicated athlete. His teammates acknowledged that while he still enjoyed goofing off, it was his intense work ethic that had propelled him to his current level of success. Flickinger was set to take his 26-7 record to the NCAA Division II Wrestling Championships at Loras College in Dubuque, Iowa, where he had also recently earned the Most Outstanding Wrestler award.

Flickinger credits his success to his commitment. "It’s all about dedication," he stated. "If you're not dedicated, you're not going to be successful." During the offseason, he worked with Duane Bastress, focusing on lifting, conditioning, and mental preparation. Flickinger noted that the mental edge gained from competing at the national championship level was key, teaching him how to concentrate on every match and use his mind to advance his skills.

According to Bastress, Flickinger was an easy pupil. "He listens to whatever the coaches say. He’s a sponge," Bastress remarked. Bastress continued to challenge and encourage Flickinger, pushing him to improve. Coach Tom Kessler, who had initially doubted his potential, later observed that Flickinger was a dedicated student of the sport.

title: saath
headline: Bermudian Springs Qualifiers Head to PIAA State Championships
summary: The Bermudian Springs wrestling team secured multiple top-five finishes at the PIAA Regional tournament, qualifying several wrestlers for the state championships in Hershey.
cleaned_text:
**Bermudian Springs Wrestling Team Qualifies for PIAA State Championships**

By TOM HUNT
Daily Record/Sunday News

The Bermudian Springs wrestling team had a successful showing at the PIAA Regional High School tournament on Saturday, earning multiple top-five finishes that qualify wrestlers for the PIAA Championships at Giant Center in Hershey next week.

The tournament was a day of mixed results, with the league contingent failing to secure a single finalist—a rarity since the event moved to suburban Reading. The day also featured the usual mix of athletic triumphs and questionable fashion choices, including some of the worst hairstyles ever seen, ranging from mop tops to bad Mohawks.

The team’s standout qualifiers included Bryce Marshall (third at 119 lbs), who secured the school’s first four-time state tournament qualification. Other qualifiers included Seth Hoffman (third at 140 lbs), Devin O'Brien (fourth at 130 lbs), and Schmelyun (fourth at 189 lbs).

Finishing third were Hanover’s Jacob Gerlach (108 lbs), Delone Catholic’s Tyler Small (112 lbs), Littletown’s Cory Beaver (173 lbs), and Eastern York’s Shane Cooley (285 lbs). Phil Slaugh (160 lbs) from Hanover and Dallas Brown (171 lbs) of Delone Catholic took fourth place, while the Biglerville duo of Shay Shive (140 lbs) and Jeff Foster (152 lbs) won fifth place matches to move on.

Bermudian Springs head coach McCollum was pleased with the quintet of qualifiers but noted how competitive the tournament was. “It could have easily gone the other way,” he said. “The kids wrestled tough in the position they were in.”

Several wrestlers had particularly strong performances. O'Brien (130 lbs) beat league rival Zach Wolford of Hanover in the consolation semifinal to earn his ticket to Hershey. Meanwhile, Schmelyun (189 lbs) made it into his career state championship, winning four straight wrestleback matches, including a major decision over Clifton Harrison of Milton Hershey in the consolation final.

Marshall (119 lbs) won four consecutive decisions in the consolation bracket, with the final one being a nail-biter over Pen Argyl’s Austin Williams. That victory marked the 155th of his stellar career, a testament to his mental toughness, McCollum noted.

Another standout was Jacob Gerlach. He joined former Nighthawk standouts Jeremy Reck and Gary Mayfield as the program’s only freshmen to qualify for states. Gerlach earned his spot with a 7-3 overtime decision in the consolation semifinal. In the final, he outdid himself against Bryan Smith of Bethlehem Catholic. Trailing 4-0 with about 15 seconds left, Gerlach registered a reversal and pulled Smith onto his back, securing a three-point nearfall for the 5-4 victory.

Nighthawk coach Terry Conover praised Gerlach’s composure, stating, “He has amazing composure for a freshman.”

headline: Hanover Area Wrestlers Shine at PIAA Regional Championships
summary: Hanover area wrestlers performed strongly at the PIAA Class AA Southeast Regional Championships, with Bermudian Springs leading the area in qualifiers and Jacob Lain helping Hanover snap a two-year drought.
cleaned_text:
Hanover Area Wrestlers Shine at PIAA Regional Championships

By GREG SWATEK
Evening Sun Sports Writer

Strong consolation performances offered some redemption for Hanover area wrestlers on a very difficult Saturday at the PIAA Class AA Southeast Regional Championships in West Lawn.

For the first time in the tournament's history at Wilson High School, the area did not send one wrestler to the finals, breaking a streak.

“The kids battled hard all week; I think they wrestled better than they did last week,” said McCollum.

Bermudian Springs, which led the area with eight regional qualifiers, will send five wrestlers to states. Hanover, Delone Catholic, andBiglerville will each send two, and Littlestown will send one.

“We have three matches Saturday to finish third at 119 and become the first dian wrestler to qualify for the PIAA Championships four times,” said Marshall, who will be seeking his third state medal.

“That means a lot to me,” said McCollum. “He finds a way to come back after tough losses; he keeps things going. There's a whole group of kids who get (to states) once and never get back. He has gotten there four times now. That’s pretty amazing.”

Other state qualifiers include Seth Hoffman, who finished third at 130; Devin O’Brien, who was fourth at 135; Derek Schmeilyun, who was third at 140; and Tyler Stambaugh, who was third at 189. Hanover snapped a two-year drought without a PIAA qualifier thanks to the efforts of freshman Jacob Lain.

“He’s very resilient,” she said.

With the injury, Lain said they hoped he would even make it. “It was really hard to recover from cartilage in his chest,” Lain said. “It was a tough start. But the first 25 meters, I was under control.”

The Rockets, Hanover area team, finished 14th place with 19 points. South Willowed in 16th with 19 points, and Gettysburg can with 19 points.

title: < + "Dave
headline: Local Wrestlers Achieve State Success
summary: Multiple local wrestlers achieved significant success at the state level, culminating in program achievements guided by coach McCollum.
cleaned_text:
The Poe Eagles Wrestling Program Sees State Success

Local wrestlers achieved significant success at the state level, with multiple athletes from Delone Catholic, Bermudian Springs, and other area schools advancing deep into the postseason.

Dallas Brown of Delone Catholic emerged in the Squire lineup during the holidays, winning the District AA 17-pound title over Beaver. He advanced to the state tournament, finishing the season with a 16-6 record.

Shane Cootay of Eastern York, a senior, reached the PIAA tournament, where he suffered two losses.

Hunter Doan, a sophomore from Delone Catholic, competed at 119 pounds, dropping down from 125 pounds. He ended up tying with all-star Bryce Marshall, who also suffered a season-ending injury.

Bryce Marshall of Bermudian Springs, the 119-pounder, capped his stellar career with a fourth pin to Hershey and a third PIAA title. He finished his career with a total of 158 victories.

Other standout performances included Eric Palc of Bigharville, a four-year starter with a 30-7 senior season, and Derek Schmetyun of Bermudian Springs, a sophomore who earned another berth in the State tournament after winning four straight co-regional titles.

Tyler Small of Delone Catholic, the 112-pound wrestler, placed ninth in the postseason, following a third-place finish in the regionals.

Tyler Stambaugh of Bermudian Springs, a junior, pulled a major upset at 189 pounds. He rode that momentum through the general championships, placing fourth to earn a spot in the state tournament.

The program was highlighted by the leadership of coach McCollum, who guided the team to the YAIAA Division I crown. The squad advanced further into the PIAA Tournament Championships than any previous Bermudian Springs team, securing five PIAA qualifiers.

title: BY JOSH MARTIN
headline: Bermudian Springs Eagles Loaded for State Competition
summary: The Bermudian Springs Eagles wrestling team, coached by McCollum, is highly successful and aiming for a top finish in the YAAA-2 league after recent victories.
cleaned_text:
**Bermudian Springs Eagles Loaded for State Competition**

By Josh Martin
Times Sports Edition

While most matspreads are looking to be already set, Bermudian Springs is loaded for beginners. Josh Martin's Eagles are deep and dangerous, and they are raring to go to play last season. The Bermudian Springs team, which went 10-0 in the District 3 Team Championships and aims to compete at the state level, is on a journey to lock up a top finish.

The Eagles have placed themselves in a strong position for the YAAA-2 league. Coach McCollum is not allowing his team to advance toward February, however.

When it comes to the team, the goal is always to win the team championship first, and then see where the individual athletes can go from there. McCollum has directed the Eagles to 344 wins over his 30 previous seasons in the YAAA-2 league. He is a contender in the league, and the team hopes to continue its winning streak.

The Eagles have already put on a show of strength by taking out two time-leading 103-AA champions, Boiling Springs, on Monday night. Boiling Springs is not a bad team, but they belonged among the team's 1AA elite last season when they won 77 matches in March.

Bermudian Springs Downs Delone Catholic in Wrestling Match

Bermudian Springs defeated Delone Catholic in a recent wrestling bout, winning with a score of 41-23. The victory was attributed by coach Dave McCollum to the team's dedication and extra practice.

“Our biggest match—by handing Delone’s Tyler Small his second loss of the season in a come-from-behind 4-2 decision—it’s Delone,” McCollum stated. “He’s sort of like an unsung hero. They came in and they just wrestled better than us tonight,” he added.

McCollum also praised the team's effort, noting that the wrestlers were always ready for Saturdays. He highlighted the importance of the team's preparation, which allowed them to secure the win.

Delone Catholic head coach John Hefner was present for the match. The article also mentioned several wrestlers involved in the action, including Josh Ruppert, Jake Boyde, Nick Blasone, and Tyler Stambaugh.

title: The Gettysburg Times
headline: Eagles Overwhelm Delone Catholic in Dominant Victory
summary: Bermudian Springs defeated Delone Catholic in a decisive wrestling match, securing an 11-0 record and a 32-14 cushion.
cleaned_text:
Eagles Improve to 11-0 with Big Win at Delone

By SH Martin, Sports Editor

There will be a day when Bermudian Springs can’t overwhelm opponents with a wave of talented wrestlers.

The Eagles rolled into McSherrystown last afternoon to face Delone Catholic in a highly anticipated match. The team showed just why they are clearly the team to beat in the YAIAA-2. Bermudian Springs won nine of 14 bouts, including multiple toss-ups, and ran away with a decisive victory.

“I wish you could do it again,” said Delone head coach John Hefner. “We wanted to win, but it’s not the end of the day. You try to figure out what the kids need to wrestle at a higher level.”

The Eagles (11-0, 6-0 Y-2) have been operating at a level all their own, thanks in large part to efforts like those given by Bryant and Nick Grim. When Delone (7-1, 7-1) shuffled its lineup in hopes of gaining ground at key weights, Peters (125) and Grim (152) responded with a pair of upsets that sealed the Squires’ fate.

Peters used two nearfall points in the third period to rally past state qualifier Tyler Small, who bumped up from 119. Small was close to working a hit if free from the bottom before Peters went for an elevator. A scramble ensued, and Peters wound up with the deciding nearfall points at the 1:30 mark. He rode tight from there, giving the Eagles an extra boost in the match.

“Peters has been said Bermudian Springs whose team trailed 10-6 before,” said McCollum. “He’s sort of an unsung hero on our team. He gives us all he’s got; he’s got a lot of heart. Once you think you have him beat, all of a sudden you’re in a match with him.”

The fact that Peters went for broke in a tied bout came as no surprise to McCollum.

“That's pretty much our game plan with Bryant,” he said. “We try to keep it close and let the match take its course, and hopefully he’ll hit one of his big moves. He picked a good night to hit it.”

The next two bouts were split—Devon Cunningham and Hunter Doan traded major decisions—before the Eagles put things away with a show of force from 140-160. Derek Schmelyun and Devin O’Brien chalked up fast falls. Grim upended Brayden Daigle at 152, and Jacob Stimer worked until the last second for a major decision that gave Bermudian Springs a cozy 32-14 cushion.

“They come into the meat of our lineup,” said McCollum. “From 140 up, we're pretty solid, and they responded. (Delone) moved on us, and I figured they would with the position they were in.”

Hefner opted to move Daigle, a former state qualifier, up to 152 in hopes of offsetting the big points scored by Schmelyun and O’Brien. Grim wrecked those plans when he was able to control the bout on the mat, twice turning Daigle, who was fighting through a shoulder injury.

headline: Bermudian Springs Clinches Tie for Division II Crown
summary: Bermudian Springs defeated Biglerville 46-9 in a highly physical match, securing a tie for the YAIAA Division II championship crown.
cleaned_text:
Bermudian Springs Beats Biglerville, Clinching Tie for Division II Crown

BIGLERVILLE – Tuesday night’s Bermudian Springs-Biglerville match featured everything one would want in a rivalry game: an overflowing crowd, the sight of the Eagles wrestlers’ shaved heads, and very physical action at times bordering on chippy.

In a raucous match, Bermudian Springs, looking to repeat as YAIAA Division II champions, won 11 of the 14 bouts to record a 46-9 blowout win over Biglerville. The victory clinched a tie for the outright division crown.

“Bermudian Springs wrestled really well. I take my hat off to them,” said Canner coach Bob Gano. “They took advantage of our youth. We got beat by a better team tonight.”

The Eagles coach, though disappointed with the final margin of victory, felt his squad may have just saved their best performance of the year for their Adams County rivals.

“I knew this could be a tough match. You don't get to be 232 without anything in your lineup,” he said, referencing the Canners’ stellar overall record. “The boys came to wrestle (Tuesday), and we had a terrific match.”

Bermudian Springs got a huge start from the duo of Jake Stimer and Nick Grim in the first two bouts of the evening. Stimer scored nine points in the last seconds of the first period to take control of his bout. Up 12-0 at the start of the final period, the Eagle senior wrestled Bobo to his back and secured a fall just 23 seconds into the third period. Grim then followed up with a workmanlike 9-2 decision over Seth Shive. Shive scored the first takedown of the bout before Grim scored nine straight, narrowly missing a major decision.

McCollum could have chosen to send Grim out against Bobo but decided Stimer would be the better choice. “That was a last-minute decision. Last year, Grim beat Shive, pinned him actually,” McCollum said. “We made the change after 10 minutes of discussing it in the locker room. We wanted to get two wins and we got that.”

Biglerville (23-3, 8-1) was also looking for two wins when it forfeited to District 3 champion Tyler Stambaugh at 189 and bumped Danny Thomas and Jeff.

title: al play-
headline: Bermudian Springs Dominates, Secures Top Seed for District Championships
summary: Bermudian Springs achieved a dominant 15-0 record and secured the top seed for the District 3-AA Team Championships, highlighted by Dalton Anthony's victory at New Oxford.
cleaned_text:
**Bermudian Springs Dominates, Secures Top Seed for District Championships**

The Gettysburg Times
Thu, Jan 24, 2008 -Page 9

[Image Caption: Bermudian Springs’ Dalton Anthony turns Hanover’s Steven Hughman with a half-nelson during their 125-pound bout]

Wednesday evening at New Oxford, Dalton Anthony of Bermudian Springs won by a 19-3 technical fall in the 125-pound bout, helping the Eagles secure a 56-15 victory.

Bermudian Springs presented yet another overwhelming piece of evidence in its case to be a No. 1 seed for next week’s District 3-AA Team Championships.

The Eagles ran away with a 15-0 overall record, which they feel should be good enough to earn them the top seed.

“They're too much for us,” said head coach Terry Conover. “We endured a 56-15 drubbing at the hands of the Eagles.”

“I think it does,” said McCollum. “Wherever they want to put us is going to be fine with me, but it’s going to be hard to argue against 15-0 and the point spread that we had on Biglerville and Schuylkill Valley, and winning three tournaments that we were in. We are deserving of a one seed. We are nitely be in the top two.”

Bermudian Springs shot out of the gates like it had a point to prove Wednesday, running up a 23-0 lead after the first four bouts.

“Clint Kramer came through for us at 215,” McCollum said. “He's very athletic and he's come a long way.”

Earlier in the day, the team was well on their way to a shutout when Nick Blasone squared off with sophomore Jacob Gerlach in a bout featuring two wrestlers with state tournament experience. Blasone scored out of a scramble early in the first period, and upped his lead to 3-0 with an escape in the third. Blasone appeared to have things locked up following a nice double-leg takedown with 22 ticks left on the clock, but Gerlach still had other ideas. The Nighthawk sophomore worked free with precious little time left and hooked up an over-under before tossing Blasone to his back. The buzzer sounded just as the official finished the nearfall count.

headline: Area Teams Lock Spots in District 1 Team Championship
summary: Area high school wrestling teams have secured spots in the District 1 Team Championship, with local coaches discussing the strong performance of their upper half of the league.
cleaned_text:
Area teams have officially locked up spots in next week's District 1 Team Championship. While the seeding for the tournament will be announced Sunday afternoon at Lower Dupont, the team can ask about their placement Wednesday at the Class AAA field. Bermudian Springs, Ridgeline, Delaware Catholic, and Lafayette have been seeded in 10A.

"Our upper half of the league is as good as any in the state," said Bermudian Springs coach McCollum. "I think our kids have shown that they're competitive."

Last season, Dylan Ferguson made history as the first Parkfield wrestler to qualify for the Division 3 Championships. Ferguson received a deserved ovation from the crowd at Delaware Catholic for his groundbreaking accomplishments in Section 1. This season, Ferguson has shared the spotlight with several teammates as Parkfield looks to qualify for the second round of the season.

Times Square Editor

When his team wrapped up its perfect season, Lance Lotus, and Diana McCollum, the wrestling coach for Bermudian Springs, were prepared to face a top seed for the District 10 championship. Apparently, McCollum's team was the only one that left the Eagles, earning the prime jumping spot for the season. The 16-seed team will be led by (15), followed by Brendan Wythe (20).

McCollum was prepared to argue his case for the number one seed on Sunday, but did not win. "I didn't a a wheel lot," he admitted. "Honest a big sale." Despite not being the highest seed, the Eagles will continue to be road warriors.

summary: Bermudian Springs is highly seeded for the PIAA Team Championships, with its wrestlers scheduled for key matches in both Class AA and Class AAA.
cleaned_text:
York Daily Record
Mon, Jan 28, 2008

**Bermudian Springs Team Set for PIAA Championship Matches**

The Bermudian Springs wrestling team is set to take its place in the 2008 District 3 Class AA and Class AAA PIAA Team Championships, which begin Tuesday.

Bermudian Springs emerged from Sunday's seeding meeting at Lower Dauphin as the top seed in the Class AA field, edging out Brandywine Heights by a score of 20-0.

In Class AAA, nationally ranked Central Dauphin holds the No. 1 spot, followed by the YAIAA Division I champion, Spring Grove.

"We call ourselves the 'Road Warriors.' Our kids expect to wrestle on the road," said Eagle coach Dave McCollum. He noted that while the team is accustomed to competing away from home, the experience of the gym-less Division II champions will be a unique challenge.

For the upcoming round, McCollum’s Class AA squad will be heading north to Cumberland Valley High School. The Bermudian Springs squad will take on the No. 16 seed Columbia, which enters the contest with a 6-12 record. Meanwhile, the host team, seeded No. 6 in Class AAA, will face Wilson, the No. 11 seed, on side-by-side mats.

"The doubleheader is going to be great for high school wrestling fans with a AA and AAA match simultaneously," McCollum said. He added that he must wait to see who wins on Tuesday before finding out where his team’s Thursday match will be held.

Biglerville and Delone Catholic were seeded fifth and sixth, giving the league three of the top six seeds.

The wrestling action was intense across multiple weight classes. In one bout, a standout 125-pounder from Red Lion was facing a tough opponent. The match was a battle of strength, with Albright noting, "Strength at 125 certainly isn't the same as strength at 135. I tried to turn him, and he posted with his arm. It was like trying to move a tree."

Another notable match involved Jayshon Wilson, a phenomenal 125-pounder, who came through with a tying reversal. Later, in the AAA 125-pounder match, Wilson held the time, sending the bout to overtime.

Carlisle's Matt McCollum achieved a milestone, recording his 100th victory with an 80-point major decision over Joel Gemmill.

Other victories included Spring Grove's Randy Abel, who pinned his opponent at 135 pounds, and Jedd Dillman, who scored the Buckskins' second victory with a 6-5 overtime win over Dylan Wisner.

Following a pin by James Yohe, the team was able to clinch the match.

Speaking about the importance of consistency, Miller advised, "Dual meets are based on bonus points, so you can't give them away, and you have to get them. That's what it comes down to."

title: District 3 boys and girls
headline: Bermudian Springs Wins District 3-AA Team Championship
summary: Bermudian Springs won the District 3-AA Team Championship, securing their third title in the event's history.
cleaned_text:
Bermudian Springs Blasts Way to District 3-AA Team Championship

By Josh Martin, Times Sports Editor

With mouths watering and stomachs empty, Bermudian Springs was dismissed from the table last season, unable to tame its hunger. A year later, the Eagles returned to Hershey hungrier than ever—and this time they devoured everything in sight.

Bermudian Springs whetted its appetite with a 36-21 semifinal win over Schuylkill Valley before crowning the District Championship Saturday at Milton Hershey’s Spartan Center. The victory marked the third team tournament title for the Eagles, who also won in 2000 and 2004. It may have been the most satisfying of the three howe because of how tantalizingly close they had come to winning the last two years.

“Two years ago we lost in the semis and we lost last year in the finals, but we just stuck in there,” said Bermudian head coach Dave McCollum. “A lot of these kids were there when we lost last year. They know what it’s like and how tough it is to win here. To come back here this year and win it is awesome.”

McCollum’s choice of superlative to describe the feeling of victory was apt when detailing his team’s performance Saturday. Against two of the premier teams in District 3, Bermudian (19-0) won 17 of 28 bouts, picking up bonus points in nine of those wins. By contrast, the Eagles gave up bonus points in just four of 28 total bouts Saturday.

“We knew we had to go out and wrestle our hearts out,” said senior Matt Spahr, who helped nail down the title with a pin. “Don’t give up. Don’t let him up, just do whatever you can to not let him up.”

“I wanted this so bad. Last year we came so close and came up short. This year there was no coming up short because this is our year,” said Tyler Stambaugh.

Seniors Clint Kramer, Tyler Stambaugh, and Spahr combined to deliver the knockout blow by sweeping the 189, 215, and 285 weight classes. The trifecta put Brandywine (23-1) on the ropes, and freshman Tanner Coyle iced the title with a fall in the penultimate bout.

title: The Gettysburg Times
headline: Bermudian Springs Aims for Top-Four Finish at PIAA Team Championships
summary: Bermudian Springs, having reached the PIAA Team Championships after a successful district run, is now focused on securing a top-four finish despite the uncertainty of their opponents.
cleaned_text:
The Gettysburg Times
Fri, Feb 08, 2008 -Page 9

Freshman Tanner Coyle helped Bermudian Springs achieve a District 3 Class AA team championship last weekend at Milton Hershey. The Bermudian Springs wrestling team simply hoped to survive long enough to wrestle four times at the PIAA Team Championships. The Eagles did just that, competing in what proved to be a grueling day of competition that saw them butt heads with four district champions.

As they prepare to step onto the mat today at Hershey's Giant Center, survival is no longer the goal. The team now aims for the top four.

“We're to the point where we're hoping to get more of the top four,” said a coach. “We've set the bar pretty high, but we want to take it a step farther. I feel like we're in the top four, but we have to wrestle hard to get there. I think we're ready and as focused as we can be.”

(Bermudian Springs, 19-0) knows it will wrestle in the Class AA quarterfinals at noon, but what the Eagles don’t yet know is who they'll face.

Coyle and the Eagles are in action today for the Team Championships at the Giant Center in Hershey. Bermudian Springs was the state runner-up last season, losing to powerhouse Reynolds in the finals, 33-26.

The uncertainty has made a web surfer out of McCollum, who has been scouring the internet in search of any and every scrap of information available pertaining to the Rangers.

“It’s really nice to have the internet,” McCollum joked Thursday night from the team’s hotel in Hershey. “That does help as far as finding out about kids, records, and who they've wrestled. It’s a little time-consuming, and we actually [use it to] make three teams because now if Ridgway would get there.”

The Eagles aim to land a top-four finish at state.

title: “Dave
headline: Bermudian Springs Dominates State Quarterfinals in Wrestling
summary: Bermudian Springs secured a strong performance in the state quarterfinals, extending their lead and advancing through multiple weight classes.
cleaned_text:
“The state quarterfinals are tough, and it only gets tougher from this point,” the coach said. “Obviously, we can’t rest. We need to stay focused for the semifinal, and hopefully our kids will do that, and we'll see what we’ve got.”

Bermudian Springs continued its winning ways, recording a pin at 160 pounds when Jake Stimer defeated Steve Zandarski in 1 minute and 34 seconds. Following that, freshman Matt Wolf secured a tight 10-9 decision at 171 pounds, extending the Eagles' lead to 18-4.

Northwestern’s Ben Clymer then pinned Evan Brechbiel in 2:47 at 189 pounds, but Tyler Stam responded with a pin of Hayden Hughes at 215 pounds.

In other bouts, Nick Blasone scored a 17-5 major decision over Eddie Faust at 112 pounds. Northwestern’s Evan Yenolevich won by technical fall over Bryant Peters at 119 pounds, before Dalton Anthony won the final bout of the night for Bermudian Springs at 125 pounds.

The Eagles also saw a forfeiture in the 130-pound bout, which was already up 36-21.

The coach added that if the Eagles win at 11 a.m. today, they qualify for the state championship match at 3 p.m. If a team loses its semifinal match, they turn around and wrestle again at 1 p.m. in the consolation semifinal round.

The Eagles reached their championship match after defeating Bedford in the semifinals. Meanwhile, the Bison entered the tournament undefeated at 170 pounds, having secured a major victory over Shady Side Academy in the quarterfinals.

One coach reflected on the effort of his team, calling the experience "almost a dream come true." He noted that the wrestlers had "wrestled their hearts out," emphasizing the high level of competition in the state finals.

In one standout bout, Speer, an Eagle junior, was known for his big moves. He capitalized on an opponent's mistake near the edge of the mat, hitting a throw and securing a fall with only 43 seconds left in the bout. This victory was a massive boost for his team and the surrounding fans.

Another wrestler shared his experience of overcoming doubt, stating, "The coaches believed in me. I just kept at my hardest."

The article also highlighted other intense matches, including a comeback victory by Stimer. In another bout, Matt McKillon, a Buccaneer senior, pinned his opponent with time remaining, stunning the bench.

The competition remained fierce, particularly in the 135-pound weight class, which featured a pair of 2007 PIAA medallists. Earlier, Welsh defeated Eagle standout Derek Schmeleyun, handing the Bermudian Springs junior a 10-2 defeat.

Following a match, McCollum advised a wrestler, stating, "You gotta stick to your basics. Against good people, you can't use your junk." Despite the team not winning the bout, the coaching staff remained focused on the fundamentals of the sport.

The Gettysburg Times
Mon, Feb 11, 2008

**State Championships and Local Sports Action**

The state championship tournament was a grueling affair, spanning nearly two full days. The competition was intense, with teams battling through the regular season, district finals, and semi-finals. One notable match included a 33-28 win over the District 5 champion, Bedford, in the semi-finals, which secured a spot in the title match.

In the wrestling arena, the coach for Bermudian Springs, McCollum, noted the dedication of the athletes. "The year we had was just incredible, almost a dream come true," he stated. "The kids came out and wrestled their hearts out." The team faced tough competition, including the 2003 champion Juniata and the District 7 champion, who refused to back down.

In other sports, the action was equally heated. The basketball game featured Elizabethtown, who began with a strong 7-2 run and finished the first quarter with a 14-7 lead. The Warriors struggled to maintain momentum, unable to take advantage of a scoring drought. Ron Johnson scored the first points of the second quarter at the 3:33 mark with a power hop, followed by an eight-foot drainer in the lane. Elizabethtown’s Quinn Cozzens was also highlighted for her performance.

title: jETTYSBURG TIMES PHOTOGRAPH
headline: Sectional Success Continues for Local Wrestlers
summary: Local wrestlers achieved multiple sectional victories, highlighted by Hunter Doan's win and Tyler Small's upset victory over Bermudian Springs.
cleaned_text:
Hunter Doan of Catholic defeated Ben Killeen of Littlestown at 130 pounds with a 5-1 decision during the D3-AA Sectional tournament on Saturday.

In another notable victory, Derek Schmelyun of Bermudian Springs defeated Zach Wolford of Hanover at 135 pounds with a 3-1 decision. Schmelyun, who wrestled at 36-3, converted a whizzer situation into a takedown midway through the second period to secure the win.

McCollum, the wrestling coach for Bermudian Springs, noted that the team had been prepared for a controlled match against quality opponents like Wolford. Schmelyun was joined atop the podium by teammates Nick Blasone (112), Devin O’Brien (145), and Tyler Stambaugh (189). McCollum also remarked that the snow delays had been a "blessing in disguise" for the team.

Meanwhile, Tyler Small, a sophomore from Squire, achieved a redemption victory at 119 pounds, defeating Bryant Peters of Bermudian Springs. Small opened the scoring with a nifty shot and, after a struggle, reversed Peters with 10 seconds left to win the match 4-3.

Earlier in the day, Junior Hunter Doan (20-1) picked up his first sectional crown, defeating Ben Killeen of Littlestown at 130 pounds with a 5-1 decision.

In other matches, James Leedy of Hanover secured his second sectional title at 152 pounds. Separately, Canner senior Smuck defeated Shay Foster of York with a lateral drop, ending Foster’s run.

title: JOHN C. WHITEHEAD, The Patriot-News
headline: Local Wrestlers Compete in District Tournaments
summary: The article reports on recent high school wrestling tournament results, including victories and setbacks, and details the annual Hall of Fame induction ceremony for the District 3 Wrestling Coaches Association.
cleaned_text:
John C. Whitehead, The Patriot-News

**Tournament Highlights and Hall of Fame Induction**

In recent bouts, Max Miller of Delone Catholic won the Class AA 189-pound bout after defeating an opponent who was reportedly getting choked.

In other tournament action, Yocco (46-4) defeated East Pennsboro’s Garrett Conrad in the quarterfinals. Yocco now faces Upper Dauphin’s Billy Mione in this morning’s semifinal round.

Matt McCollum, the senior wrestler, faced a tough challenge. After recovering from an injury, McCollum was able to return to the mat, but an opponent reversed and cradled him, an effort that was saved by the buzzer. Although McCollum had to use the remaining injury time, he eventually defaulted when he was too injured to continue wrestling. McCollum subsequently forfeited his consolation match with South Western’s Seth Wherley, concluding a productive wrestling career for the son of the head coach.

In a display of revenge, Bryan Pearsall of Warwick pinned Central Dauphin’s Tyler Buckman in the 125-pound quarterfinals. This victory was particularly meaningful, as last year Pearsall had held a handy lead over CD’s Kenny Stank in the 119-pound semi-finals before Stank struck with a sudden and fatal trip.

At the state tournament level, Middletown freshman Shane Miller was one of two runners-up in Friday’s 112-pound quarterfinals. Spring Grove’s Cory Baker, last year’s silver medalist at 171 pounds, was also knocked off the mat, but he had a good excuse. He was pinned in 3:39 by last year’s 160-pound champion, Susquehanna Twp.’s Edward Ruth (22-0).

Finally, the District 3 Wrestling Coaches Association held its annual "Newport Night" ceremony to honor its 2008 Hall of Fame inductees. One-half of the six inductees either are from or have a connection to Newport: 1993 PIAA Class AA champion Damian Jones; veteran official Larry Reigle, a Newport graduate; and former Newport coach Earl Heisey. Also inducted that night were Neil Barnes and another individual connected to Dallas.

The Patriot News
Saturday, October 23, 2008

**Sports Roundup: Wrestling, Football, and Hall of Fame Inductees**

The day’s sports coverage featured multiple events, including a signal from Jenningson’s Max Miller to the referee, which was called by Dionne Obama’s Dallas Classics coach.

In wrestling, Yooco recorded a significant performance, tallying 125 pins, surpassing Mark Smith’s total. Meanwhile, Bermudian Springs coach McCollum was involved in a match against the Boston Bruins, concluding a segment on a team sponsored by Bernie Sanders.

Another highlight was the "Revenge Factor," where Bryan Porral of Warwick connected with Newport Griffin and former Newport Griffin veteran Damien Johnson.

The day concluded with "Newport Night," celebrating a Coaches Association 2008 Hall of Fame inductee.

The Gettysburg Times
Mon, Feb 25, 2008 -Page 12

**Bermudian Springs Wrestling Team Advances to Regional Tournament**

The article details several wrestling matches and concludes with the announcement that the Bermudian Springs team has qualified for the Southeast Regional Tournament.

In one match, Small was awarded a point when Yocco was penalized for stalling at the 34-second mark. Small's teammate, Shive, worked free and attempted a big move but was headlocked, giving Kuhn a 15-6 major decision.

Another bout featured O’Brien (38-4) facing defending champion Joe Harper (32-0) of Susquehanna. After a solid start to their title bout, O’Brien was forced to the corner when he became ill. Battling both Harper and dry heaves proved to be too much in a two-set match.

In a separate match, Stambaugh was defeated by Brandywine’s Brad Stoudt, 4-3. Stoudt (44-6) opened the scoring by finishing a takedown on the edge of the mat in the first period. “To get to this point and get eight through is awesome,” McCollum said. “You're wrestling the best. It’s a shame we came up a point and a half short, but I thought we had a great weekend overall.”

The Bermudian Springs wrestlers who qualified for regionals include Tanner Coyle (103), Nick Blasone (112), Devin Cunningham (130), Nick Grim (152), and Matt Spahr (275).

title: “Dave
headline: Central Wins D3 Title; McCollum Named Coach of the Year
summary: Manheim Central won the District 3 wrestling championship after defeating Cumberland Valley in overtime, while McCollum was named Coach of the Year and several coaches were inducted into the District 3 Hall of Fame.
cleaned_text:
On Tuesday, February 26, 2008, the District 3 wrestling championships concluded with Manheim Central becoming the first D3 champion. Marshall Peppelman defeated Cumberland Valley’s Toey Napoli in overtime, securing the title. During the tournament, Peppelman was previously clipped by CV’s Bryce Busler in the 140 finals, and Manheim Central’s Jordan Enck suffered his first loss since the previous year’s state finals. Meanwhile, Governor Mifflin’s Chris Carabello won a match at 215 with a 7-4 victory.

In related news, Bermudian Springs coach McCollum was named the Coach of the Year for his team’s remarkable season, which included winning the D3 championship and finishing second in the PIAA Team Championships.

The day also featured the District 3 Hall of Fame induction ceremony at Hersheypark Arena. Biglerville head coach Bob Gano was honored for his 19-year career at Delone Catholic and Biglerville. Additionally, Hanover’s Terry Conover was recognized prior to the finals, marking his retirement after 37 years as a head coach. The ceremony also honored former Delone Catholic state champion Tony Koontz and former Biglerville standout Shain Leggett, who had won the AA 103-pound title in 1999.

title: <7 "Dave
headline: Brandywine Heights Claims District 3 Wrestling Championship After Scoring Error
summary: Brandywine Heights won the District 3 Class AA team championship after a scoring error involving team points for fifth and sixth place was corrected.
cleaned_text:
On Saturday night at Hershey Park Arena, Brandywine Heights scored a dramatic victory to claim the District 3 Class AA team championship. The victory was secured when Brandywine’s Brad Stoudt took down defending champion Tyler Stambaugh of Bermudian Springs late in the 189-pound final.

The tournament was held at Wilson High School for the Class AA Southeast Regional. The scoring of the tournament was complicated by a software error. According to former District 3 chairman Randy Umberger, the software used by the District 3 pairmaster, former West Perry athlete Demonte “Spike” Temple, did not add in the team points for wrestlers who placed fifth and sixth.

The error was brought to Umberger’s attention by McCollum, the wrestling coach for Bermudian Springs. McCollum had flu-like symptoms over the weekend and had not closely followed the scoring, but he asked his assistants to double-check the scoring on Sunday.

Umberger read the contents of an email sent by an Escape Sports representative to The Patriot-News. In the email, the representative acknowledged that while Temple applied the software properly, the system failed to calculate team points for fifth and sixth-place finishers.

The corrected scores showed that Brandywine Heights had outscored Bermudian Springs 157-150, reversing the initial calculation.

title: CHRIS KNIGHT, The Associated Press.
headline: Scoring Error Gives Bermudian Springs District 3 Wrestling Title
summary: A scoring error in the District 3 Class AA wrestling tournament, which failed to count points for fifth and sixth place, allowed Bermudian Springs to claim the team championship instead of Brandywine Heights.
cleaned_text:
On Saturday night at Hershey Park Arena, Brandywine Heights scored a dramatic victory to claim the District 3 Class AA team championship.

It appeared the four team points earned by Brandywine's Brad Stoudt, who took down defending champion Tyler Stambaugh of Bermudian Springs late in the 189-pound final, gave the Bulldogs a 136-135 edge and their second straight 3A-AA title.

Just one problem: Brandywine didn’t win the team title. Bermudian Springs did.

A problem with the software used by District 3 to score the tournament didn’t add in the team points for fifth and sixth place finishers, points that would have given Springs a 157-150 edge, according to former District chairman Randy Umberger.

Umberger read the contents of an email sent by an Escape Sports representative to The Patriot-News in which the representative acknowledged that the District 3 pairmaster, former West Perry athletic director Demonte Temple, applied the points properly, but that the software failed to register the team points for fifth- and sixth-place finishers.

The error was brought to Umberger’s attention by McCollum. McCollum had flu-like symptoms over the weekend and did not closely follow the scoring, but asked his assistants to double-check the details of the Times story. The assistants reported that Bermudian Springs had outscored Brandywine Heights 157-150, the same numbers that the software calculated once the points were properly added.

cleaned_text:
On Saturday night at her skypark Arena, Brandywine Heights scored to claim the District 3 Class AA team championship. The victory was secured by four team points earned by Brandywine Boudou, who defeated Tyler Sambourg of Bermudian Springs late in the 189-pound weight class. This win gave Boudou its second straight AA title. The article notes that the team's success was notable, especially considering that the team did not win the championship in other sports like basketball or softball. Furthermore, the piece discusses a potential scoring discrepancy in the tournament, suggesting that certain points awarded by District 3 might not have been accurately counted in the final score against Bermudian Springs.

headline: Bermudian Springs Wrestlers Shine at State Tournament
summary: Bermudian Springs wrestlers Devin O'Brien and Derek Schmelyun earned multiple medals at the state tournament, demonstrating strong individual and team performance despite facing physical setbacks.
cleaned_text:
**Bermudian Springs Wrestlers Shine at State Tournament**

Bermudian Springs wrestlers had a strong showing at the state tournament, with both Devin O'Brien and Derek Schmelyun earning multiple medals and showcasing impressive resilience.

Devin O'Brien, a Bermudian junior, overcame a bout of illness and fatigue to finish third in the 145-pound weight class. After battling through a first-round loss and dealing with flu symptoms, O'Brien recovered and pinned two of his next three opponents to secure a spot in the consolation semifinals, where he defeated Joe Harper in a 158-point decision. His third-place match against Reuben Herrera was a grueling affair, requiring O'Brien to win in the second overtime period after hooking Herrera’s leg and holding him down for the decisive 30 seconds. Coach McCollum praised O'Brien’s performance, noting that the junior showed remarkable determination.

Meanwhile, the Bermudian Springs squad brought home three medals overall. Derek Schmelyun dominated his division, defeating Troy High School’s Devon Leiby in the 135-pound weight class to claim third place. Schmelyun’s performance was highlighted by his ability to secure victories in multiple matches, keeping him close to a state title appearance.

In other wrestling action, the semifinal round featured a hard-fought contest between Albright and Sherlock. After a scoreless opening, the match remained tied until the second period. The bout was highly physical, with both wrestlers exchanging takedowns and maintaining control until the final minutes.
